01 - Conversion
70% above target (1.7K vs. 1K) - but the pipeline still has its challenges.

The conversion funnel reveals where the real opportunities are.

Drop 1 - Reach
39%

never see it

Of 75K eligible users, only 46K have been exposed to Wix Checking. The remaining 39% simply haven't encountered the product yet.

Drop 2 - Discovery
72%

aren't pulled in

72% of exposed users never reach the Welcome Screen. The entry points aren't visible or compelling enough to drive discovery.

Current entry point
Drop 3 - Intent
77%

don't commit

77% of users who see the Welcome Screen never click Open Account. Something holds them back from taking the first step.

Welcome screen
Drop 4 - Execution
44%

overwhelmed by the process

Of users who click Open Account, 44% drop off during SSN verification, business info, and card steps. Only 56% complete the flow.

Onboarding flow prototype (Figma)

Four drops. Four problems. One strategy.

Every drop in the funnel maps to a distinct problem. Two are top-of-funnel reach problems. Two are onboarding product problems.

Each gets its own escalation stage.

02 - Usage Evidence
Early signs: once onboarded, users stay and engage deeply
Preliminary data from existing users shows strong engagement. Product value is evident in how users manage their funds.
55%
GPV in Wix Checking
Over half of total GPV managed directly through Wix Checking - proving trust and daily utility.
52%
All-In Users
Over half of active users manage >80% of their business earnings in Wix Checking.
10%
Pass-through Only
A limited segment uses it solely as a gateway. Most users find deep product value beyond pass-through.

98-100%
35%
68% of users manage 50% or more of their earnings inside Wix Checking. 35% are fully committed (98-100%). The distribution is bimodal: users either go all-in or barely use it.
The bottleneck is not value proposition - it's friction in active commitment. H1 focus is conversion at scale. In H2, we shift to also deepening usage and engagement.
03 - Why This Matters
Wix Checking vs. Legacy Payouts
Legacy
Payout Constraints
  • −Scheduled payouts to external bank accounts
  • −Slower liquidity and cash access
  • −Fragmented financial visibility
  • −No real-time spending capabilities
Wix Checking
Advantage
  • ✓Instant access to earnings
  • ✓Unified money management
  • ✓Real-time spending with business debit cards
  • ✓Manual payouts on demand
The Strategy

"Earn the Default"

Instead of hiding the change, we own it. A graduated path of education, consent, and escalation - earning trust stage by stage.

04 - Education, Consent & Escalation
Earning the default, stage by stage
Each stage addresses specific psychological barriers and funnel drops. We only escalate when the previous stage proves safe.
Eligible Users
75K
-39%
Drop 1
Exposed

Drop 4
Account Created
1.7K
Drop 1Drop 2
Stage 0 - Continuous

Increase Exposure & Discovery

Barriers this stage addresses
Money Behavior Change
Funds appear in a new account instantly rather than following the familiar external payout flow. Users experience disorientation when their money lands somewhere unexpected.
Considered - Auto Payouts
100% of incoming funds are automatically flushed out to the user's external bank account. Removes the anxiety - but users keep the same scheduled payout behavior and never experience Checking's value.
What we'll do instead
Educate about the change. Transparent communication about where funds go and why, before it happens.
Familiarity Bias
Users are comfortable with existing payouts. Even when the new option is objectively better, the preference is to avoid change.
What we'll do
Proactive education. Transparent communication about what's changing and why. Users make informed decisions instead of fearful ones.
Bank Account Creation Anxiety
Users worry about credit score impact or hidden regulatory obligations. The word "account" triggers complexity and commitment.
Considered - Wallet Framing
Rebrand as a "wallet." But requires significant technical effort, doesn't educate users, and we still need consent regardless of framing.
What we'll do instead
Redesigned welcome screen. Clear, transparent language about what Wix Checking is, how funds will behave, and what changes for the user - so the choice feels informed, not intimidating.

Running in parallel across all stages:

  • Marketing campaigns
  • VIP user outreach
  • New entry points
  • Eligibility expansion
  • Embed in Wix general flows
Drop 3Drop 4 - Execution
Stage 1

One-Click Setup

Barrier this stage addresses
Debit Card Commitment
Physical card issuance feels like an unasked-for lock-in, even when provided at no cost.
What we'll do
Virtual-first default. No physical card issued by default. Lowers psychological weight while keeping full utility.

"Greasing the slide" - removing the friction from the process entirely.

  • Scope: One-click activation with consent UX. Security steps moved to post-onboarding. Virtual card by default.
  • Goal: Eliminate the 44% verification drop-off.
  • Risk: Accidental activation - "I didn't mean to open an account."
  • Measure: Objection vs. Clarification ratio.
One-click setup demo
G
Whenever ready - gradually roll out removal of onboarding steps.
Drop 1Drop 2Drop 3
Stage 2

Clear Choice, Active Decision

Barriers this stage addresses
Money Behavior Change
Funds appear in a new account instantly rather than following the familiar external payout flow. Users experience disorientation when their money lands somewhere unexpected.
Considered - Auto Payouts
100% of incoming funds are automatically flushed out to the user's external bank account. Removes the anxiety - but users keep the same scheduled payout behavior and never experience Checking's value.
What we'll do instead
Educate about the change. Transparent communication about where funds go and why, before it happens.
Familiarity Bias
Users are comfortable with existing payouts. Even when the new option is objectively better, the preference is to avoid change.
What we'll do
Proactive education. Transparent communication about what's changing and why. Users make informed decisions instead of fearful ones.
Bank Account Creation Anxiety
Users worry about credit score impact or hidden regulatory obligations. The word "account" triggers complexity and commitment.
Considered - Wallet Framing
Rebrand as a "wallet." But requires significant technical effort, doesn't educate users, and we still need consent regardless of framing.
What we'll do instead
Redesigned welcome screen. Clear, transparent language about what Wix Checking is, how funds will behave, and what changes for the user - so the choice feels informed, not intimidating.

No more procrastination - every user makes a clear, informed decision.

  • Experience: Triggered on entry into FMG screens. No skip option - active choice is mandatory (either "Legacy Payouts" or Wix Checking).
  • Goal: Force an active decision. Gather insights on "No" reasoning.
  • Timing: By then, Stage 1 has made the "Yes" path effortless.
Active choice screen Checking dashboard
G
Enough users have chosen, flow was clear, no real objections. We understand the intent of those staying with legacy.
Drop 1Drop 2Drop 3
Stage 3

Pending Payouts

Closing the loop for inactive users who haven't moved.

  • Scope: External payouts are blocked. Pre-notice and KYC pre-validation deployed ahead.
  • Goal: Final push for remaining inactive users before deprecation.
  • Measure: Support volume & sentiment. Zero abnormal churn or GPV shock.
G
If remaining users are only explained by "change avoidance" (not real objections), we consider the endgame.
Drop 1Drop 2Drop 3Drop 4
Stage 4

Full Deprecation

The moment where we choose product value over legacy comfort.

  • Standard: Wix Checking becomes the sole standard experience. External payouts remain only for non-eligible users.
  • Scope: Includes auto-payouts to mitigate behavior change for remaining legacy users who transitioned.

The path is clear.

We've proven the value. We've identified the friction. Now it's time to make Wix Checking the default.
